model_narrative: This is a planning model for an integrated Mexican steel industry made up of mines, raw-material plants, and steel mills serving a set of domestic market areas. We decide how intensively to run each production process at every mine, raw-material plant, and mill; how much of each commodity to ship along every leg of the network from mines to plants to mills to markets, including interplant transfers; how much of each input to buy domestically or import; and how much finished product to export or bring in from abroad. The goal is to minimize the total cost of the whole system, which combines recurrent operating and purchasing costs, transport costs across the network, and the net cost of imports against export revenue converted at the shadow exchange rate.

sets.name: im
sets.doc: iron-ore and coal mining locations

sets.name: ir
sets.doc: raw-material plants that turn mined inputs into intermediate raw materials such as pellets and coke

sets.name: iss
sets.doc: integrated steel mills, the main production sites

sets.name: j
sets.doc: domestic market areas where finished steel is demanded

sets.name: pm
sets.doc: production processes available at the mines

sets.name: pr
sets.doc: production processes available at the raw-material plants

sets.name: ps
sets.doc: production processes available at the steel mills

sets.name: cm
sets.doc: commodities handled at the mines

sets.name: cr
sets.doc: commodities handled at the raw-material plants

sets.name: cs
sets.doc: commodities handled at the steel mills, spanning ores, intermediates, finished products and purchased inputs

sets.name: cf
sets.doc: final finished steel products sold to markets

sets.name: ce
sets.doc: commodities that may be exported

sets.name: o
sets.doc: ownership shares in the Pena Colorada pellet mine, one entry per owner

sets.name: mm
sets.doc: productive units (equipment groups) at the mines

sets.name: mr
sets.doc: productive units at the raw-material plants

sets.name: ms
sets.doc: productive units at the steel mills

params.name: km
params.doc: available capacity of each productive unit at each mine, in thousands of tons per year

params.name: kr
params.doc: available capacity of each productive unit at each raw-material plant, in thousands of tons per year

params.name: ks
params.doc: available capacity of each productive unit at each steel mill, in thousands of tons per year

params.name: d
params.doc: required deliveries of each final product to each market area, in thousands of tons per year

params.name: emax
params.doc: maximum allowed exports of each final product, in thousands of tons per year

params.name: etot
params.doc: overall ceiling on total exports across all products, in thousands of tons per year

params.name: sh
params.doc: shadow exchange rate used to convert foreign-currency import and export flows into the cost accounting

vars.name: zm
vars.doc: operating level of each mining process at each mine

vars.name: zr
vars.doc: operating level of each process at each raw-material plant

vars.name: zs
vars.doc: operating level of each process at each steel mill

vars.name: xm
vars.doc: shipments of mined commodities from a mine to a downstream raw-material plant or steel mill; only physically allowed origin-destination combinations are present

vars.name: xr
vars.doc: shipments of commodities from a raw-material plant to a steel mill

vars.name: xs
vars.doc: interplant shipments of commodities from one steel mill to another

vars.name: xf
vars.doc: shipments of finished products from a steel mill to a market area

vars.name: ur
vars.doc: domestic purchases of input commodities made at the raw-material plants

vars.name: us
vars.doc: domestic purchases of input commodities made at the steel mills

vars.name: e
vars.doc: exports of finished products shipped abroad from a steel mill

vars.name: vs
vars.doc: imports of input commodities delivered to a steel mill

vars.name: vf
vars.doc: imports of finished products delivered directly to a market area to help meet demand

vars.name: cost
vars.doc: total system cost, in millions of US dollars; the quantity being minimized

vars.name: recurrent
vars.doc: recurrent operating cost: process operating costs plus the cost of domestically purchased inputs, in millions of US dollars

vars.name: transport
vars.doc: total transport cost over every shipment leg in the network, in millions of US dollars

vars.name: imp
vars.doc: total cost of imported commodities and finished products, in millions of US dollars

vars.name: exp
vars.doc: total revenue earned from exports, in millions of US dollars

constraints.name: xm['ore-n','la-perla','ahmsa']
constraints.intent: At each mine, the production of every commodity must be enough to cover everything that leaves the mine. For each commodity at each mine, the amount produced by the mining processes there must be at least the total amount of that commodity shipped out to the downstream plants and mills.

constraints.name: zr['coke-hd','esperanzas']
constraints.intent: At each raw-material plant, the supply of every commodity must cover what is sent onward. For each commodity at each plant, the amount produced by the plant's processes plus what arrives from the mines plus what is purchased domestically must be at least the total amount of that commodity shipped out to the steel mills.

constraints.name: zs['sinter','ahmsa']
constraints.intent: At each steel mill, the availability of every commodity must cover all of its uses. For each commodity at each mill, the amount made on site plus everything brought in from mines, raw-material plants, other mills, domestic purchases, and imports must be at least the total of that commodity sent to other mills, delivered to markets, and exported.

constraints.name: zm['min-co','coahuila'])
constraints.intent: Mining capacity cannot be exceeded. For each productive unit at each mine, the total use of that unit across all processes running there must not exceed its available capacity.

constraints.name: zr['pelt-c','penacol'])
constraints.intent: Raw-material plant capacity cannot be exceeded. For each productive unit at each raw-material plant, the total use of that unit across the processes running there must not exceed its available capacity.

constraints.name: zs['pelt-c','sicartsa'])
constraints.intent: Steel-mill capacity cannot be exceeded. For each productive unit at each steel mill, the total use of that unit across the processes running there must not exceed its available capacity.

constraints.name: xf['plate','ahmsa','mexico-df']
constraints.intent: Every market's demand for each final product must be met. For each final product in each market area, the total delivered from the steel mills plus any of that product imported directly into the market must be at least the required amount.

constraints.name: e['plate','ahmsa']
constraints.intent: Exports of each product are limited. For each final product, the total amount exported across all mills must not exceed the export limit set for that product.

constraints.name: me2
constraints.intent: Overall exports are capped. The total of all exports, summed over every product and every mill, must not exceed the overall export ceiling.

constraints.name: xr['pellets','penacol','sicartsa'])
constraints.intent: Each owner's pellet take from the Pena Colorada mine is limited by its ownership share. For each owner, the pellets shipped from that mine to mills it has a stake in must not exceed that owner's share of the mine's pellet capacity.

constraints.name: pelal
constraints.intent: The Alzada site ships no pellets. The total pellet shipments out of Alzada to the steel mills must be zero.

constraints.name: acost
constraints.intent: Total cost is assembled from its parts. The total cost equals the recurrent cost plus the transport cost plus the net of import cost minus export revenue, with that net foreign-currency amount converted using the shadow exchange rate.

constraints.name: arec
constraints.intent: Recurrent cost accounts for running processes and buying inputs. The recurrent cost equals the operating cost of all mining processes plus the cost of every input commodity purchased domestically at the raw-material plants and at the steel mills.

constraints.name: atrans
constraints.intent: Transport cost adds up the cost of every shipment in the network. The transport cost equals the sum over all shipment legs of the per-unit transport cost on that leg times the amount shipped, covering moves from mines, between plants and mills, to markets, and to and from the ports for imports and exports.

constraints.name: aimp
constraints.intent: Import cost values everything brought in from abroad. The import cost equals the sum over all imported input commodities and imported finished products of the import price times the quantity imported.

constraints.name: aexp
constraints.intent: Export revenue values everything shipped abroad. The export revenue equals the sum over all exported products of the export price times the quantity exported.

query: We're sketching out an export-quota scenario — total steel shipped abroad held to no more than 150 thousand tons. What does the optimizer return for total cost under that ceiling?
expected_pyomo: model.export_ceiling = Constraint(expr=sum(model.e[c, s] for c in model.ce for s in model.iss) <= 150)

query: There's interest in reducing how much finished steel we lean on imports for. If we held total imports of final products to 900 thousand tons, where does total cost land?
expected_pyomo: model.vf_cap = Constraint(expr=sum(model.vf[c, j] for c in model.cf for j in model.j) <= 900)
